outbound_official.notes: No official Algeria government outbound visa-list page located. The MFA (mfa.gov.dz) 'services-for-algerians' section covers only civil status, document legalization, emergency passports, deregistration certificates, T@SDIK, and the DZ Travellers emergency registration platform (apcs.mfa.gov.dz) — the latter is a crisis/emergency traveler registration form, not a visa entitlement list. No page analogous to India's MEA VFFIN exists on any official Algerian government domain. All outbound access data for Algerian passport holders appears only on third-party aggregator sources (Henley, Wikipedia, visa-agency sites). Rely on destination-side data.

visa_free.notes: Visa-free for ordinary passport holders. Max stay 90 days per entry; cumulative stay may not exceed 180 days per year.
Visa-free for ordinary passport holders. Also listed in diplomatic/service passport exemptions on Oslo embassy page.
Visa-free for ordinary passport holders. Also listed in diplomatic/service passport exemptions on Oslo and Dublin embassy pages.

Visa-free for ordinary passport holders per London CG. Note: Dublin embassy list omits Morocco — London CG is the primary public-facing source for ordinary passport exemptions and explicitly includes Morocco.

Visa-free for ordinary passport holders. Listed as 'Sahrawi Arab Democratic Republic' / 'Arab Sahrawi Democratic Republic' across official Algerian sources. This is a disputed territory; inclusion reflects the official Algerian government listing.

visa_policy.notes: All non-Algerians require a visa except for citizens of: Libya, Malaysia, Maldives, Mali, Mauritania, Morocco, Sahrawi Arab Democratic Republic, Seychelles, and Tunisia (ordinary passports). Diplomatic passport-only exemptions: Argentina, Croatia, France, Germany, Hungary, Poland, Romania. Diplomatic or service/official passport exemptions: Albania, Benin, Brazil, Cuba, Egypt, Ethiopia, Greece, Guinea, Italy, Malaysia, Mali, Malta, Mexico, Niger, Peru, Portugal, Republic of Korea, Senegal, Slovakia, South Africa, Spain, Switzerland, Tanzania, Turkey, United Arab Emirates, Venezuela, Vietnam, Belarus. A regularization visa (visa on arrival) exists only for organized tours to southern Algeria (all nationalities). Citizens of Hungary and Slovenia (ordinary passports) and holders of a residence permit from those countries may obtain a regularization visa on arrival for southern Algeria without prior embassy visa (southern provinces only, organized tour required). No e-visa or ETA system is in place. Visa validity periods: 90 days, 180 days, 1 year, or 2 years. Max stay per entry: 90 days; cumulative: 180 days per year.

conditional_access.conditions: Citizens of Hungary and Slovenia wishing to travel for tourist purposes for a period not exceeding one month to specific southern Algerian provinces (Adrar, Laghouat, Biskra, Bechar, Tamanrasset, Djelfa, Msila, Ouargla, El-Bayad, Illizi, Tindouf, El-Oued, Naama, Ghardaia, Timimoune, Bordj Badji Mokhtar, Ouled Djellal, Beni Abbes, Ain Salah, Ain Guezzam, Touggourt, Djanet, El M'Ghaier, El Menia) are not required to apply for a tourist visa at the Embassy level. Instead, they must obtain a Boarding Authorisation with a QR Code from an Algerian Accredited Travel Agency approved by Algerian Competent Authorities. A regularization visa is obtained upon arrival at airports or sea/land border crossing points. Tourist visa fees are paid on arrival. Applies to southern provinces only; entry visa arrangements for non-southern city destinations remain unchanged.

credential.subtype: any valid residence permit issued by Hungary

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of a valid residence permit issued by Hungary, wishing to travel for tourist purposes for a period not exceeding one month to specific southern Algerian provinces (Adrar, Laghouat, Biskra, Bechar, Tamanrasset, Djelfa, Msila, Ouargla, El-Bayad, Illizi, Tindouf, El-Oued, Naama, Ghardaia, Timimoune, Bordj Badji Mokhtar, Ouled Djellal, Beni Abbes, Ain Salah, Ain Guezzam, Touggourt, Djanet, El M'Ghaier, El Menia) are not required to apply for a tourist visa at the Embassy level. Instead, they must obtain a Boarding Authorisation with a QR Code from an Algerian Accredited Travel Agency approved by Algerian Competent Authorities. A regularization visa is obtained upon arrival. Tourist visa fees are paid on arrival. Applies to southern provinces only; entry visa arrangements for non-southern city destinations remain unchanged.

credential.subtype: any valid residence permit issued by Slovenia

Instead, they must obtain a Boarding Authorisation with a QR Code from an Algerian Accredited Travel Agency approved by Algerian Competent Authorities. A regularization visa is obtained upon arrival. Tourist visa fees are paid on arrival. Applies to southern provinces only; entry visa arrangements for non-southern city destinations remain unchanged.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program found on any official Algerian government source. Searches of mfa.gov.dz and related official domains returned no CBI program.

validation_notes: 2026-06-13 re-validation: Live-scraped Oslo (2024), London CG (2023), Dublin (2023), Budapest (2023), Washington (2026), and Pretoria (2023) embassy pages via Firecrawl and WebSearch.

ADDED: New conditional_access entry for Hungary and Slovenia CITIZENS (ordinary passports) for southern Algeria regularization visa — the Budapest press release covers 'Citizens from Hungary and Slovenia as well as holders of a residence permit issued by these countries'; the prior record only captured the residence-permit-holder angle, missing the nationality-based component.

ADDED: Malaysia and Mali diplomatic/service passport entries to conditional_access — Oslo embassy explicitly lists both in the diplomatic+service exemption table alongside their ordinary-passport visa-free status.
CONFIRMED: Argentina and Poland appear in diplomatic-only list on Oslo embassy page; Dublin page does not list them but Oslo is authoritative — prior record was correct.

CONFIRMED: Morocco remains in visa_free (ordinary passport) per London CG explicit listing; Dublin omits Morocco but London CG is a primary public-facing source for ordinary passport exemptions — no correction made.
CONFIRMED: Belarus in diplomatic+service list per Dublin embassy — prior record correct.
CONFIRMED: Regularization visa (VoA for organized southern tours) confirmed on Oslo page with same list of wilayas — no change to that entry.
CONFIRMED: No e-visa, no ETA system in place per all official sources checked.
CONFIRMED: No CBI, RBI, or fast_track programs on any official source.
gaps: Morocco is in the London CG visa-free list but absent from the Dublin list — minor inter-post discrepancy; London CG is the primary UK-facing official source and is used here.
The Dublin embassy omits Argentina and Poland from the diplomatic-only list; Oslo includes them. Minor inter-post discrepancy; Oslo used as source.

Max stay days for diplomatic/official passport exempt categories not explicitly stated on official source; the general 90-day-per-entry rule applies to regular visas but is not confirmed for diplomatic/official passport holders.
No official Algerian government source confirms an e-visa or ETA system.
RBI and fast-track programs: no official English-language source found; may exist in domestic legislation or Arabic-language publications.
Algeria is a member of the Arab Maghreb Union (AMU), which calls for eventual free movement, but the AMU has been largely inactive. No operationally active free-movement agreement is in force.
Dual citizenship policy for Algeria not found on official English-language MFA pages.
The Sahrawi Arab Democratic Republic is a disputed territory; its inclusion in the visa-free list reflects the official Algerian government listing.
The Budapest press release for Hungary/Slovenia does not specify the date the measure took effect.

Claims that holders of valid US, Canada, UK, or Schengen visas/residence permits (beyond Hungary and Slovenia) can enter Algeria visa-free or obtain a VoA appear only in aggregator sources — NOT confirmed on any official mfa.gov.dz page verified during this research. Not recorded pending official confirmation.
OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder exemption for Algeria not found on any official Algerian government source.

The MFA main 'Work and Residence in Algeria' page (mfa.gov.dz/services-for-foreigners/work-and-residence-in-algeria) returned no content — formal residence permit and long-term residence details could not be verified from official source.
